introduction:
A Marriage Certificate in Maharashtra is a vital legal document that serves as definitive proof of marriage. It is officially registered by the Marriage Registrar under the Hindu Marriage Act, 1955 or the Special Marriage Act, 1954.

Obtaining a marriage certificate is essential for various legal procedures, including applying for a passport, opening joint bank accounts, claiming life insurance benefits, and establishing spousal rights.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Processing Time: Typically 7-15 days for Hindu Marriage Act after application submission (30-day notice required for Special Marriage Act).
  • Application Fee: ₹100 as registration fee under the Hindu Marriage Act and ₹150 under the Special Marriage Act. Additional nominal facilitation fees apply online.
  • A Marriage Certificate is valid for a lifetime.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Marriage Registration

Note: You need to provide documents for Proof of Age, Proof of Address, Photographs, and Witnesses.
Proof of Identity & Age
  • Birth Certificate
  • SSC Certificate
  • Passport
Proof of Address
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID
  • Ration Card, or Passport
Evidence of Marriage
  • Joint photograph of the couple in wedding attire
  • Photograph during the ceremony
  • Original wedding invitation card
Photographs & Witnesses
  • Passport-size photographs of bride and groom
  • Three witnesses with their Identity and Address Proofs (Aadhaar/Voter ID)
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via Aaple Sarkar Portal

Maharashtra allows online marriage registration through the Aaple Sarkar portal. Pre-filling details online reduces office wait time and ensures a smooth single-visit experience at the Registrar.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Official Portal
    Go to aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in. This is the single Government of Maharashtra citizen services portal.
  2. Register / Login
    Click "New User? Register Here". Enter your Aadhaar number and mobile number. Verify via OTP and set a password. If already registered, simply log in with your credentials.
  3. Select the Marriage Registration Service
    After login, navigate to Revenue & Forest Department (or search "Marriage"). Click on "Registration of Marriage (Hindu Marriage Act / Special Marriage Act)".
  4. Fill Application Form D
    Enter all required details accurately — husband's full name, wife's full name (as on Aadhaar), date of marriage, place of marriage, religion, and full details of all three witnesses including their Aadhaar numbers.
  5. Upload Scanned Documents
    Upload clear scanned copies (JPG/PDF, max 200 KB each): age proof (Birth Certificate or SSC), address proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID), joint wedding photograph, ceremony photograph, and wedding invitation card.
  6. Pay Fee & Schedule Appointment
    Pay ₹100 (Hindu Marriage Act) or ₹150 (Special Marriage Act) online via the payment gateway. An Acknowledgement Slip with Appointment Date will be sent via SMS and email. Save this slip — it is mandatory for your Registrar visit.
  7. Visit the Registrar on Appointment Day
    Both spouses and all three witnesses must be physically present at the Sub-Registrar of Assurances office on the given date. Carry all original documents for verification and signing of the marriage register.
Tip: After successful registration, download your digitally signed Marriage Certificate directly from Aaple Sarkar portal using your application reference number.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Sub-Registrar of Assurances / Ward Office

You can walk in directly to your local Sub-Registrar of Assurances office or Ward Office / Gram Panchayat to apply without internet access. Working hours: Monday – Friday, 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Locate the Correct Office
    Visit the Sub-Registrar's office under whose jurisdiction the marriage was solemnized or where either spouse has resided for at least 6 months. You can find your nearest office via igrmaharashtra.gov.in.
  2. Collect Form D
    Ask for Form D (Application for Marriage Registration) from the counter. The form is provided free of cost. Alternatively, you may download and print it from Aaple Sarkar ahead of time.
  3. Fill the Form in BLOCK LETTERS
    Use black or blue ink and BLOCK LETTERS. Enter: full names of husband and wife (as per Aadhaar), date and place of marriage, religion, names and Aadhaar numbers of three witnesses, and residential address of both spouses.
  4. Attach Self-Attested Photocopies
    Attach: age proof (Birth Certificate / SSC marksheet), address proof (Aadhaar / Voter ID / Ration Card), joint wedding photograph, 2 ceremony photographs, wedding invitation card. Bring originals for counter verification.
  5. Collect Signatures from All Parties
    The husband, wife, and all three witnesses must sign the form in front of the Registrar. The officiating priest's signature may also be required under the Hindu Marriage Act.
  6. Submit at Counter & Pay Fee
    Hand over the completed form and document set. Pay ₹100 at the cash counter. The officer issues an Acknowledgement Receipt with your registration number.
  7. Receive Certificate
    The Marriage Certificate is issued on the same day or within 7–15 working days depending on the office. Collect it from the office or download it from Aaple Sarkar once available.
Processing Time: Approximately 7–15 working days. For grievances, contact the Maharashtra Stamp Duty & Registration helpline: 020-25501107.
afterApplying:
After Registration - What Next?

Physical Presence Verification

Both spouses and witnesses must physically appear before the Marriage Registrar at the designated municipal/panchayat office to sign the registry.

Certificate Issuance

Upon successful verification of original documents, the formal Marriage Certificate is generated, signed, and made available for download.
